jokes.text: Gee, I hope there ain't nobody down there getting busy.
jokes.setup: Will announces he's going downstairs to the family room
jokes.punchline: Hopes nobody is 'getting busy' down there - sexual innuendo about his aunt and uncle
jokes.explanation: Good character-appropriate line for Will's street-smart personality, but relatively predictable sexual innuendo setup

jokes.text: Look, man, y'all are married. Where y'all put your earrings is your business.
jokes.setup: Philip explains Vivian's earring rolled under the couch
jokes.punchline: Will interprets this as continued sexual activity reference
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that extends the innuendo while maintaining Will's misunderstanding

jokes.text: In theory, that probably means that you can't run, either. Right?
jokes.setup: Philip says he can't stand up from his back injury
jokes.punchline: Will logically deduces Philip can't run either, setting up his confession
jokes.explanation: Clever setup showing Will's opportunistic thinking - he's calculated the perfect moment to confess something bad

jokes.text: that I wore that earring to school today.
jokes.setup: Will's careful setup about Philip not being able to run
jokes.punchline: Reveals he wore Vivian's earring to school - the big confession
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the setup - perfectly timed confession when Philip is helpless, with great character logic

jokes.text: Get him, Vivian.
jokes.setup: Philip learns Will wore Vivian's earring but can't get up due to back injury
jokes.punchline: Helplessly asks Vivian to chase Will for him
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper to the sequence - Philip's helpless frustration is both funny and relatable

jokes.text: It is definitely peach season, man.
jokes.setup: Carlton shows off his peach-colored formal outfit
jokes.punchline: Will comments on the excessive peach color like it's a seasonal fruit reference
jokes.explanation: Solid visual gag callback about Carlton's excessive formal wear choices

jokes.text: I would prefer an assassin's bullet to this kind of living hell.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks Geoffrey to clean something for him
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey dramatically prefers death to serving the family
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey line - perfectly over-the-top British butler complaint delivered with deadpan precision

jokes.text: Yo, I think G's drawers must be bunching again.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey delivers his dramatic complaint about serving the family
jokes.punchline: Will reduces Geoffrey's existential complaint to underwear discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Geoffrey's drama through Will's street-level interpretation

jokes.text: I did, but she had to wash her hair.
jokes.setup: Will suggests Carlton ask Sinead O'Connor to the dance
jokes.punchline: Carlton says she had to wash her hair (she's famously bald)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist joke - the classic 'washing hair' excuse applied to a bald celebrity. Perfect economy and surprise.

jokes.text: I know they don't walk in LA, but I at least hoped they talked.
jokes.setup: Will is speechless upon seeing Dee Dee
jokes.punchline: Dee Dee makes a cultural observation about LA stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Nice character establishment for Dee Dee showing her wit and directness

jokes.text: Can you do that and walk at the same time?
jokes.setup: Carlton says 'Hi' in his typical formal manner
jokes.punchline: Dee Dee questions if he can handle basic multitasking
jokes.explanation: Standard 'you're awkward' joke but fits Dee Dee's direct personality

jokes.text: with big ears and weird clothes. I mean, not just you, anybody.
jokes.setup: Dee Dee describes what's wrong with blind dates - tall, skinny guys
jokes.punchline: Realizes she's describing Will exactly, tries to backtrack awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment where Dee Dee accidentally insults Will then tries to recover

jokes.text: I'll be the one in the seat next to you... shouting my phone number to Magic Johnson.
jokes.setup: Will says maybe he'll see her at the game
jokes.punchline: Dee Dee reveals her bold plan to hit on Magic Johnson
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Dee Dee's confidence and directness - specific and memorable

jokes.text: I hope Magic ate his Wheaties.
jokes.setup: Dee Dee just said she'll shout her phone number to Magic Johnson
jokes.punchline: Carlton hopes Magic is prepared for Dee Dee's aggressive approach
jokes.explanation: Nice dry callback that plays on the Wheaties 'breakfast of champions' slogan

jokes.text: Why does this seem so familiar?
jokes.setup: Vivian complains Philip just lies on his back giving orders while she does all the work
jokes.punchline: Realizes this dynamic sounds familiar (sexual innuendo)
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual innuendo joke but well-delivered by Vivian's character

jokes.text: You're clowning me, right, Uncle Phil?
jokes.setup: Philip suggests Will ask Dee Dee to the dance
jokes.punchline: Will thinks Philip must be joking about asking the overweight girl
jokes.explanation: Sets up Will's prejudice about Dee Dee's weight - necessary for character arc but not particularly funny

jokes.text: I haven't heard of nothing that disgusting... since Clarence Thomas found that hair on his cola.
jokes.setup: They watch an infomercial for a gross-sounding chili sandwich maker
jokes.punchline: Will compares it to the famous Clarence Thomas/Anita Hill controversy
jokes.explanation: Topical reference to a major 1991 scandal - edgy for family sitcom and well-integrated into the scene

jokes.text: Here you have a movie set in Africa... there's only one brother in the whole thing, and he's from Detroit.
jokes.setup: Philip defends Casablanca as the best movie ever
jokes.punchline: Will points out the lack of Black actors in a movie set in Africa
jokes.explanation: Smart social commentary wrapped in humor - legitimate criticism of classic Hollywood delivered with Will's perspective

jokes.text: Shut your mouth. I'm talking about Shaft. Then we can dig it.
jokes.setup: Will mentions liking the movie Shaft
jokes.punchline: Philip responds with the famous Shaft theme song lyrics as dialogue
jokes.explanation: Fun reference to Isaac Hayes' Shaft theme song that shows Philip's unexpected coolness

jokes.text: Yes. I'm about to laugh myself into a seizure.
jokes.setup: Will asks Geoffrey if he doesn't think their Shaft exchange was funny
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey sarcastically claims he's about to have a medical emergency from laughing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey line - dry British sarcasm delivered with medical precision

jokes.text: Just put it on and say, 'Boss, da plane, da plane.'
jokes.setup: Carlton's tux shrunk and now fits too small
jokes.punchline: Will suggests he dress as Tattoo from Fantasy Island
jokes.explanation: Solid pop culture reference joke about Carlton's height and ill-fitting formal wear

jokes.text: Why don't I just go pitch a tent under a freeway... and eat cold beans with a stick?
jokes.setup: Will suggests Carlton rent a tux instead of wearing his own
jokes.punchline: Carlton dramatically equates renting formal wear to homelessness
jokes.explanation: Great Carlton line showing his privileged perspective - perfect character comedy

jokes.text: I got as far as, 'Yo, yo, baby. Yo, baby, yo.'
jokes.setup: Will is trying to get his 'rap' together to ask Claudia out
jokes.punchline: His pickup line is just repetitive 'yo's and 'baby's
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's usually smooth persona faltering

jokes.text: and I know she ain't got your baby.
jokes.setup: Dee Dee points out Will's pickup line problems - she's not named 'Yo'
jokes.punchline: And she definitely doesn't have his baby
jokes.explanation: Sharp logical deconstruction of Will's weak pickup line by Dee Dee

jokes.text: How about that big old zit on your nose?
jokes.setup: Carlton asks what else could go wrong with his dance preparation
jokes.punchline: Will points out Carlton has a pimple on his nose
jokes.explanation: Lazy punchline - just mean-spirited observation with no real wit

jokes.text: But I'm worried about her cholesterol level.
jokes.setup: Vivian says Dee Dee is cute and fun
jokes.punchline: Will expresses fake concern about her health due to weight
jokes.explanation: Reveals Will's prejudice but not particularly clever - just mean-spirited

jokes.text: Hell, I'm going to the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Carlton's friends make awkward comments about Dee Dee
jokes.punchline: Dee Dee bluntly announces her bathroom trip to escape the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Great Dee Dee moment - direct and honest response to uncomfortable situation

jokes.text: Of course, that one would be hard to lose.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Dee Dee being 'tons of fun'
jokes.punchline: Cornflake makes a weight joke about her being hard to lose
jokes.explanation: Cheap, mean-spirited weight joke with no cleverness - purely cruel humor

jokes.text: I can't figure out how a brother goes from T-bone to rump roast.
jokes.setup: Ty is comparing Will's dating choices in meat terms
jokes.punchline: Compares attractive women to T-bone steak and Dee Dee to rump roast
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay structure but ultimately just another cruel weight joke

jokes.text: And I ain't afraid to do it, neither.
jokes.setup: Will threatens to call Dee Dee back if she doesn't pick up
jokes.punchline: Acts like calling back is a serious threat he's brave enough to carry out
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of a trivial action into false bravado

jokes.text: I'll put some chili in his pocket.
jokes.setup: Philip wants Geoffrey to make a chili-pocket sandwich with the Sandwich King
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey threatens violence instead of making the sandwich
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey line - transforms food service into threat with butler's precision

jokes.text: A pimple?
jokes.setup: Carlton's date Kelly cancels for a medical reason
jokes.punchline: The 'medical emergency' is just a pimple
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for Carlton's vanity about appearance - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: A great girl.
jokes.setup: Carlton wonders what kind of girl is home without a date on Saturday night
jokes.punchline: Dee Dee appears right on cue to answer his question
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy - Dee Dee's entrance answers Carlton's question with confidence

jokes.text: Wow, have you sold the movie rights yet?
jokes.setup: Claudia tells a boring story about wearing white after Labor Day
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically suggests her mundane story deserves film adaptation
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcastic response to Claudia's privileged complaints - good character contrast

jokes.text: No, you got a sort of a Lou Rawls thing going there, man.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks if he doesn't look as bad as he thinks in his ill-fitting tux
jokes.punchline: Will compares him to smooth soul singer Lou Rawls (who was also short)
jokes.explanation: Nice reference that's both complimentary and gently mocking of Carlton's appearance

jokes.text: Well, it ain't like we brothers or nothing. Beat it, C.
jokes.setup: Will realizes Claudia doesn't want to sit with Carlton
jokes.punchline: Will abandons Carlton to impress his shallow date
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's moral compromise to fit in - uncomfortable but necessary character moment

jokes.text: I wonder if you could hang yourself with a cummerbund.
jokes.setup: Carlton is left alone, rejected by everyone at the dance
jokes.punchline: Contemplates suicide using formal wear accessories
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny line that perfectly captures Carlton's despair in character-appropriate formal wear terms

jokes.text: I can walk! It's a miracle.
jokes.setup: Philip has been stuck on his back with injury
jokes.punchline: Suddenly recovers and acts like it's a religious miracle
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy recovery with religious miracle reference

jokes.text: Yeah, like jamming a fork in your hand... you know, just to make sure you're still alive.
jokes.setup: Claudia asks if he can imagine how she felt about her friend's shoe choice
jokes.punchline: Will's darkly sarcastic comparison to self-harm
jokes.explanation: Will's perfectly calibrated sarcasm about Claudia's trivial concerns. Dark humor that goes over her head completely.

jokes.text: Cornflake, come on, come with me, man. You push her in. I'll flush.
jokes.setup: Will is frustrated with Claudia's boring conversation
jokes.punchline: Suggests drowning her in the toilet with Cornflake's help
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's more mean than funny - shows Will's frustration but lacks wit

jokes.text: Where'd you find him at? Rent-a-sucker?
jokes.setup: Will sees Dee Dee with her date Robert
jokes.punchline: Suggests Robert came from a rental service for losers
jokes.explanation: Good jealous reaction from Will - economical insult that shows his territorial feelings

jokes.text: Strawberry.
jokes.setup: Claudia asks what color Will really likes
jokes.punchline: Will says 'strawberry' - not even a real color
jokes.explanation: Absurdist response that confuses Claudia - shows Will's mental disconnection from the conversation

jokes.text: My kind of honey.
jokes.setup: Dee Dee says she'll wear anything that buttons and is clean
jokes.punchline: Will realizes Dee Dee's practicality is more appealing than Claudia's vanity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's realization about what he values in a partner

jokes.text: If I can button it, and it's clean, I'll pretty much wear anything. / My kind of honey.
jokes.setup: Will asks if Dee Dee would wear white after Labor Day
jokes.punchline: Her practical response contrasted with Claudia's pretentious concerns, Will's realization
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast showing Dee Dee's down-to-earth practicality versus Claudia's shallow pretensions. Will's moment of clarity.

jokes.text: This clever device is probably... I can't believe we didn't get in on that abdominizer.
jokes.setup: Will and Dee Dee are now watching infomercials together in bed
jokes.punchline: They regret not buying previous infomercial products
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Will and Dee Dee's compatibility through shared interests

jokes.text: I'm trying to hear the musical toilet seat.
jokes.setup: Dee Dee asks Will and her to be quiet
jokes.punchline: Will wants to hear an absurd infomercial product - a toilet seat that plays music
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist infomercial product that sounds ridiculous enough to be real

jokes.text: Other than that, man, I mean, I like her.
jokes.setup: Carlton lists all the disasters from his evening at the dance
jokes.punchline: Despite everything terrible, he still likes his date
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton optimism - finds the positive despite obvious disasters
